title: Labour Audits
content: **[LABOUR AUDITS]{.underline}**
A. Service Overview

A Labour Audit is the best starting point for any business. It shows you exactly where you stand today, what risks you're facing, and what needs to be fixed first . Think of it as a health check for your business---it highlights problems before they become expensive disasters and gives you a clear plan to move forward .
B. Detailed Description

A Labour Audit reviews everything related to your staff and compliance. We check your contracts, policies, payroll, record-keeping, and processes to find gaps and risks . At the end, you'll know exactly where you stand and what to do next .
Why a Labour Audit is the right starting point:
🔹 It tells you where you are -- Many businesses think they're fine until something goes wrong. An audit shows you the reality of your situation .
🔹 It identifies your risks -- We find the problems that could cost you money, time, or your reputation .
🔹 It prioritizes what matters -- Not everything needs to be fixed at once. We tell you what's urgent and what can wait .
🔹 It gives you a clear plan -- You'll get a step-by-step action plan showing exactly what to do and in what order .
🔹 It sets you up for success -- Once you know your priorities, you can work towards real goals instead of guessing .
What we review in a Labour Audit:
✔ Employment contracts -- Are they legal? Do they protect you? Are all the important details included?
✔ Workplace policies -- Do you have the right policies in place? Are staff actually following them?
✔ Payroll and payments -- Are you registered with SARS, UIF, and SDL? Are you paying correctly?
✔ Leave and working hours -- Are you giving the right leave? Are working hours legal?
✔ Record-keeping -- Do you have the documents you need? Can you find them if an inspector asks?
✔ Hiring and firing -- Are you following the law when you bring people in or let them go?
✔ Performance management -- Do you have a fair system to measure and manage performance?
✔ Health and safety -- Are your workplaces safe? Do you have the right policies and committees?
✔ Employment Equity -- Are you meeting your obligations if you have 50 or more employees?
✔ Training records -- Are you submitting the required training plans and reports?
What you get at the end:
🔹 A clear report showing what's working and what's not
🔹 A list of risks so you know where trouble could come from
🔹 A priority action plan telling you what to fix first
🔹 Ready-to-use documents like contracts, policies, and forms you can start using immediately
🔹 Peace of mind knowing you're prepared for inspections, disputes, or audits
**C. Who Is This For?**
✅ Small business owners who don't have HR staff and want to know if they're doing things right
✅ Growing businesses that need to get their house in order before problems start
✅ Companies facing a Department of Labour inspection and unsure if they'll pass
✅ Businesses preparing for tenders, BEE verification, or investor reviews
✅ Private schools managing staff without dedicated HR support
✅ Anyone who's never had their HR checked by a professional
✅ Businesses with high staff turnover, complaints, or problems that keep happening
**D. Why Choose OptiHR for This Service?**
🚨 Legal expertise you can trust -- Our owner is an admitted attorney with Labour Court rights, so our advice is backed by law .
🚨 We don't just find problems---we fix them -- You'll get ready-to-use documents, not just a list of what's wrong .
🚨 We make it simple -- No jargon, no confusion. We explain everything in plain language so you understand exactly what to do .
🚨 We've seen it from both sides -- We've done audits for businesses and represented employers during government inspections, so we know what to look for .
**E. Case Study or Testimonial**
A Small Business That Thought It Was Fine---Until It Wasn't

A family-run business with 30 staff thought they were doing everything right. They had contracts, they paid salaries on time, and they'd never had complaints. But when one employee was dismissed and went to the CCMA, everything unraveled .

The employee's lawyer pointed out that the contracts didn't include notice periods. There was no written disciplinary policy. Leave calculations were wrong. The CCMA case was heading towards a big payout .
That's when they called OptiHR. We did a full Labour Audit and found even more problems:
❌ Half the contracts were missing important legal clauses
❌ No proper grievance process
❌ Leave records were inconsistent
❌ Required posters weren't displayed (a Department of Labour violation)
❌ Performance reviews existed on paper but were never done

We gave them compliant contracts, proper policies, and a clear system to follow. The CCMA case settled for far less than expected. Six months later, they passed a Department of Labour inspection with no issues .
"We had no idea how many gaps we had. OptiHR didn't just tell us what was wrong---they gave us everything we needed to fix it. Now we actually know where we stand." -- Business Owner
**F. Compliance & Legal Considerations**
A Labour Audit checks if you're following South African labour laws, including :
📌 Basic Conditions of Employment Act (BCEA) -- Contracts, working hours, leave, pay
📌 Labour Relations Act (LRA) -- Disciplinary processes, dismissals, grievances
📌 Employment Equity Act (EEA) -- Fair representation, equity plans, reporting (if you have 50+ employees)
📌 Skills Development Act -- Training plans, training reports, SDL payments
📌 Occupational Health and Safety Act -- Safe workplaces, safety committees
📌 COIDA -- Workplace injury insurance and reporting
📌 UIF -- Unemployment insurance contributions
📌 POPIA -- Protecting employee personal information
📌 National Minimum Wage Act -- Paying the correct minimum wage
**G. Pricing or Engagement Models**
Full Labour Audit
A complete review of your entire business covering all compliance areas, with a detailed report, action plan, and ready-to-use documents .
Best for: First-time audits, preparing for inspections, or getting your full compliance picture.
Focused Audit
A targeted review of one specific area like contracts, policies, or payroll only .
Best for: Addressing a known problem or working within a smaller budget.
Ongoing Compliance Support
Regular check-ins throughout the year to keep you compliant as laws change, with an annual full audit .
Best for: Businesses that want to stay ahead of problems and never worry about compliance again.
Pricing depends on your business size and what you need. Contact us for a quote that fits your situation.
**H. FAQ (1--3 for each service)**
Q1: How long does a Labour Audit take?
Most audits take 2--4 weeks depending on how many employees you have and how complex your business is. Smaller businesses can often be done in 1--2 weeks .
Q2: What if the audit finds serious problems?
We'll tell you what's urgent and what's not. You'll get a clear plan showing what to fix first, and we'll give you the documents and support you need to fix things quickly .
Q3: Why is a Labour Audit a good starting point?

Because it shows you exactly where you stand right now. You can't plan where you're going if you don't know where you are. The audit identifies your risks, highlights your priorities, and gives you a clear roadmap to follow . It's like getting a map before starting a journey---you'll know which direction to go and what obstacles to avoid.
**I. Call-to-Action**
Start with clarity. Know where you stand.
A Labour Audit is the smartest first step any business can take. It shows you your risks, highlights your priorities, and gives you a plan to move forward with confidence .
👉 Book Your Free 30-Minute Consultation Now and find out where your business stands before problems find you.
